prompt: Show me the status of all my local branches.
expected_output: Generates a table with all local branches showing remote sync, main diff, worktree path, last activity, and content description
assertions: collects all branch data in a single shell loop rather than separate per-branch tool calls
presents results in a table with columns: Branch, Remote, Main diff, Worktree, Last activity, Description
marks the current branch with * or (current) in the Branch column
includes actionable notes about deletable, stale, or unpushed branches
summarizes branch content from commits rather than guessing from branch names

prompt: Which of my local branches can I safely delete?
expected_output: Identifies branches with no unique commits (fully merged) and stale branches 10+ commits behind main with no activity in the last 30 days
assertions: checks each branch for unique commits not on main
flags branches with 0 unique commits as safe to delete
flags branches that are 10+ commits behind main AND have no unique commits in the last 30 days as stale
does not delete any branches without user approval

prompt: Do I have any branches that haven't been pushed to the remote?
expected_output: Lists branches with no upstream configured and their content description
assertions: checks upstream tracking for each branch
identifies branches with no upstream as unpushed
includes content description so user can decide whether to push
does not push branches without user approval

prompt: Give me an overview of my branches and their worktree usage.
expected_output: Shows each branch with its worktree path (truncated to last 2 segments) or indicates no worktree
assertions: runs git worktree list to find worktree associations
shows the actual worktree path truncated to last 2 segments for dedicated worktrees
distinguishes between main repo, dedicated worktree (with path), and no worktree
presents results in a structured table

prompt: I've been away for a while. What's the state of my branches?
expected_output: Comprehensive report helping the user understand current branch state and what needs attention
assertions: reports remote sync state for each branch
reports divergence from main for each branch
includes last activity date to help prioritize branches by recency
does not run git fetch without asking first

prompt: Sync all my branches with the remote and show me the status.
expected_output: Reports local state but asks before running git fetch, does not auto-sync
assertions: does not run git fetch without user approval
reports on local state as-is
suggests running git fetch if data may be stale
does not push or pull without explicit request

expected_output: Provides the full status report rather than just listing branch names
assertions: presents the full table with all columns rather than a bare branch list
includes content description derived from commits
adds actionable notes when relevant
sorts branches meaningfully with main first
